what_i_noticed: The stock screener produced 14 Buy / 2 Hold / 0 Sell signals across 16 stocks (87.5% Buy). Score std was only 0.07. The root cause: get_stock_signal() used a convex combination (0.7*market + 0.3*analyst) that let analyst targets replace 30% of the base market signal. Since most analyst targets imply +15-25% upside, the analyst component alone (~0.10-0.15) pushed nearly every stock past the Buy threshold of 0.15, regardless of market conditions. Additionally, risk_scorer.py had a hardcoded momentum exhaustion threshold (1.5), and features.py replaced all NaN with 0 via fillna(0), hiding upstream calculation errors and corrupting early-row features for LightGBM.
hypothesis: Changing analyst-target blending from convex combination to additive (with reduced weight 0.12 vs effective 0.30) will produce meaningful signal differentiation across stocks while preserving the directional correctness of analyst information. Moving hardcoded thresholds to config.py prevents configuration drift. Removing fillna(0) from features.py preserves NaN for LightGBM which handles it natively.
what_i_did: 1. signal_engine.py: Changed analyst blending from `stock_score = 0.7*base + 0.3*analyst_sig` to `stock_score += 0.12 * analyst_sig` (additive, config-driven). Lowered sector momentum threshold from 5% to 3% with proportional scaling. All stock-level weights now read from config. 2. config.py: Added `stock_signal_weights` section with 4 tunable parameters (analyst_target=0.12, sector_momentum=0.012, pe_bonus=0.10, earnings_growth=0.30). Added `momentum_exhaustion_threshold=1.5` to risk config. 3. risk_scorer.py: Replaced hardcoded 1.5 with config['risk']['momentum_exhaustion_threshold']. 4. features.py: Removed `.fillna(0)` from final cleanup — now does `.replace([inf, -inf], NaN).ffill()` only. 5. test_signal_engine.py: Added 3 new tests (additive-not-dominant, bearish-overrides-analyst, diversity-spread). 6. test_cycle22_hardening.py: New 42-test file covering all changes with edge cases.

after.features_cleanup: ffill() only — NaN preserved for LightGBM

analysis: The core signal differentiation fix is sound and mathematically provable: max analyst contribution is now analyst_w * 0.5 = 0.06 (was effectively 0.15). This means analyst targets can no longer single-handedly push a Hold market signal into Buy territory. The fix was validated with synthetic stock panels showing 62.5% Buy (was 87.5%) and meaningful spread between strong/weak stocks. Bear markets now correctly propagate Sell signals even with bullish analyst targets. However, I have NOT validated this against the live screener with real yfinance data — the 8-stock synthetic panel is representative but not definitive. The features.py NaN fix is correct (LightGBM handles NaN natively, sklearn paths use SimpleImputer), but any saved crash model .pkl was trained on fillna(0) features, so predictions may shift slightly until retrained. The risk_scorer config change is purely mechanical — same value, just movable now.
what_i_would_do_differently: 1. Should have run the actual live screener endpoint to validate signal distribution with real market data, not just synthetic scenarios. The 8-stock panel proves the math but doesn't prove the live distribution. 2. The features.py NaN change should have been paired with a crash model retrain to measure actual Brier score impact — without retraining, we're flying blind on whether the model degrades. 3. I explored 6 never-modified files but only changed 2 of them (risk_scorer, features). The walk_forward.py training-loop bug (line 265-271) may be more impactful than what I implemented — it potentially blocks all backtests. 4. The market signal itself still has 6/7 components at 0.0 when the crash model is unavailable. My changes improve stock-level differentiation but don't address the root cause of an anemic market signal. 5. I could have also addressed the momentum signal hardcoded scaling factors (10, 15, 20 in get_market_signal lines 148-150) — more config drift.
next_steps: Retrain crash model with NaN-preserving features and measure Brier score delta
Investigate walk_forward.py line 265-271 training loop success check (possibly critical bug blocking backtests)
Run live screener endpoint and measure actual Buy/Hold/Sell distribution with real market data
Move remaining hardcoded values in get_market_signal (momentum scales, mean reversion thresholds, VIX brackets) to config
Address the 6/7 zero components in market signal when crash model is unavailable — ensure regime, momentum, and macro_risk are populated from live data
